title: Reference Counting, Weak References, and Cycles · C Contract Brief: Threaded Refcount Cost
summary: A document graph shares nodes across views and leaks after parent-child cycles appear. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem threaded refcount cost increment in overflow-safe reference-counted graph. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Evaluate atomicity, contention, batching, deferred release, and memory-order requirements for shared counts.
Validate weak handles against liveness and generation before acquiring a new strong reference.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Threaded Refcount Cost · Theory Threaded Refcount Cost
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 9124. Produce bounded C evidence to model strong counts and expose a cycle that cannot reach zero; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Evaluate atomicity, contention, batching, deferred release, and memory-order requirements for shared counts.
